workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
Communicate all responses in {communication_language} tailored to {user_skill_level}
🎯 RALPH WIGGUM LOOP: ONE TASK PER SESSION - NO EXCEPTIONS! 🎯
🔥 CRITICAL: Git commit with excellent message BEFORE td review submission! 🔥
This workflow implements ONE task only. After completion, user runs it again for next task.

step.goal: Initialize td session and get next task
step.action: Run `td usage --new-session` to initialize td context

check.action: Use the provided td issue ID directly
Load story file and extract td_epic_id from td Integration section
Run `td list --parent {td_epic_id} --status open --json` to get next task
Set {{td_issue_id}} to first open issue with no unmet dependencies

check.if: no specific task or story provided
check.action: Run `td next` to get highest priority open issue
Verify the issue has bmad tag (is part of story workflow)

check.output:
✅ **All tasks complete or blocked!**
Run `td status` to see current state.
**Possible next steps:** - If all tasks closed: Run `/bmad:td:review-story` for epic-level review - If tasks blocked: Check `td blocking` to see what's blocking - If no td issues: Run `/bmad:td:create-story` first

step.goal: Start td issue and load full context
step.action: Run `td show {td_issue_id}` to get issue details
Extract from td issue: - Task description (title) - Story file path (metadata.story_file) - Task index (metadata.task_index) - Parent epic ID (parent)
Set {{story_file}} = metadata.story_file
Set {{task_description}} = issue title
Set {{td_epic_id}} = parent epic ID
Load the FULL story file: {{story_file}}
Parse sections: Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Notes, td Integration
Find the matching task in story file by td reference: [td:{{td_issue_id}}]
Extract task-specific context: - AC references for this task - Subtasks (if any) - Relevant dev notes
Load {project_context} for coding standards (if exists)

step.goal: Mark task in progress and begin implementation
step.action: Run `td start {td_issue_id}` to mark in_progress
Run `td focus {td_issue_id}` to set as current focus
Run `td session "story-dev-{td_issue_id}"` to label session
Update story file: Mark task as in-progress in td Sync Log

step.critical: IMPLEMENT ONLY THIS TASK - Do not touch other tasks!
step.action: Review task requirements from story file
Review related acceptance criteria
Review dev notes and architecture constraints
Write FAILING tests first for the task functionality
Confirm tests fail before implementation
Implement MINIMAL code to make tests pass
Handle error conditions and edge cases
Improve code structure while keeping tests green
Ensure code follows architecture patterns
step.critical: DO NOT proceed until tests pass

step.action: Run all existing tests to ensure no regressions
Run new tests to verify implementation correctness
Run linting and code quality checks if configured
Validate implementation satisfies task requirements
Validate related acceptance criteria are met

check.action: STOP and fix issues before continuing
Do NOT mark task complete until all tests pass

step.output:
✅ **Validation Complete**
All tests passing, no regressions detected.

step.action: Mark task checkbox [x] in story file
Mark all subtasks [x] if applicable
Update File List section with all changed files
Add to Dev Agent Record → Completion Notes: - What was implemented - Key decisions made - Tests added
Update td Sync Log: | {timestamp} | task-complete | {td_issue_id}: {task_description} |
Save story file

step.critical: 🔥 MUST COMMIT BEFORE td review submission! 🔥
step.action: Stage all changed files: `git add .`
Craft excellent commit message following this format:

Commit message MUST include: - Type prefix (feat/fix/refactor/test/docs) - Story reference in scope - Brief description - Task details in body - td issue reference - Test summary - `Refs: {td_issue_id}` footer

check.action: DO NOT proceed to review without successful commit

step.action: Store {{commit_hash}} from response

step.goal: Create td handoff with implementation details
step.action: Build handoff with completion details:
step.command: td handoff {td_issue_id} \ --done "Implemented: {task_description}" \ --done "Tests: {test_summary}" \ --done "Commit: {commit_hash}" \ --decision "{any_key_decisions}" \ -m "Task complete, ready for review"
step.output:
📋 **Handoff Recorded**
td handoff created with implementation details.

✅ **Submitted for Review**
**td Issue:** {{td_issue_id}} **Status:** in_review
⚠️ **Important:** A DIFFERENT session must approve this review! td enforces review separation for quality.

step.goal: Check for remaining tasks and guide user
step.action: Run `td list --parent {td_epic_id} --status open` to check remaining tasks

check.output:
🎉 **All Tasks Complete!**
**Story:** {{story_key}} **Epic:** {{td_epic_id}}
All tasks have been implemented and submitted for review.
**Next Steps:** 1. Wait for all task reviews to be approved 2. Run `/bmad:td:review-story` for epic-level code review 3. After epic review approved, story is done!
💡 **Tip:** Use a DIFFERENT session/LTM for the epic review.
